class="markdown prose dark:prose-invert w-full wrap-break-word light markdown-new-styling"><p data-start="0" data-end="139">“MCP” can mean a lot of different things depending on context—but right now, especially in tech/AI discussions, it most commonly refers to:</p>
<h3 data-start="141" data-end="180"><span role="text">👉 <strong data-start="148" data-end="180">Model Context Protocol (MCP)</strong></span></h3>
<p data-start="181" data-end="216">This is an emerging standard in AI.</p>
<ul data-start="218" data-end="789">
<li data-start="218" data-end="408">
<p data-start="220" data-end="408"><strong data-start="220" data-end="235">Definition:</strong> A way for AI models (like ChatGPT or other LLMs) to connect to external tools, data, and services in a consistent, standardized way. <span class="" data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center select-none relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill" style="width: 74px;"><a href="https://www.cloudera.com/resources/faqs/model-context-protocol-mcp.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://www.cloudera.com/resources/faqs/model-context-protocol-mcp.html?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! select-none" style="max-width: 74px;"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between overflow-hidden" style="opacity: 1; transform: none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">Cloudera</span></span></span></a></span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="409" data-end="568">
<p data-start="411" data-end="568"><strong data-start="411" data-end="430">Simple analogy:</strong> It’s like a <strong data-start="443" data-end="464">USB-C port for AI</strong>—one universal connector instead of lots of custom integrations. <span class="" data-state="closed"><span class="ms-1 inline-flex max-w-full items-center select-none relative top-[-0.094rem] animate-[show_150ms_ease-in]" data-testid="webpage-citation-pill" style="width: 73px;"><a href="https://graphite.com/guides/what-is-mcp?utm_source=chatgpt.com" target="_blank" rel="noopener" alt="https://graphite.com/guides/what-is-mcp?utm_source=chatgpt.com" class="flex h-4.5 overflow-hidden rounded-xl px-2 text-[9px] font-medium transition-colors duration-150 ease-in-out text-token-text-secondary! bg-[#F4F4F4]! dark:bg-[#303030]! select-none" style="max-width: 73px;"><span class="relative start-0 bottom-0 flex h-full w-full items-center"><span class="flex h-4 w-full items-center justify-between overflow-hidden" style="opacity: 1; transform: none;"><span class="max-w-[15ch] grow truncate overflow-hidden text-center">Graphite</span></span></span></a></span></span></p>
</li>
<li data-start="569" data-end="789">
<p data-start="571" data-end="592"><strong data-start="571" data-end="590">Why it matters:</strong></p>
<ul data-start="595" data-end="789">
<li data-start="595" data-end="642">
<p data-start="597" data-end="642">Lets AI access files, databases, APIs, etc.</p>
</li>
<li data-start="645" data-end="701">
<p data-start="647" data-end="701">Avoids building separate integrations for every tool</p>
</li>
<li data-start="704" data-end="789">

prompt:

what is mcp
completion:
ChatGPT said:

“MCP” can mean a lot of different things depending on context—but right now, especially in tech/AI discussions, it most commonly refers to:

👉 Model Context Protocol (MCP)

This is an emerging standard in AI.

  • Definition: A way for AI models (like ChatGPT or other LLMs) to connect to external tools, data, and services in a consistent, standardized way. Cloudera

  • Simple analogy: It’s like a USB-C port for AI—one universal connector instead of lots of custom integrations. Graphite

  • Why it matters:

    • Lets AI access files, databases, APIs, etc.

    • Avoids building separate integrations for every tool

    • Makes AI systems more powerful and flexible Bandwidth

Example:
Instead of coding a custom connection between an AI and Google Drive, Slack, and a database, MCP lets the AI use all of them through one shared protocol.

⚠️ Other meanings of “MCP”

If you saw it somewhere else, it might mean something different, like:

  • “Male chauvinist pig” (slang) merriam-webster.com

  • A medical or scientific term

  • A degree (Master of City Planning)

  • A company, airport code, or technical system Wikipedia

✅ Bottom line:

  • In AI/tech → almost always Model Context Protocol

  • In casual slang or other fields → could mean something totally different

If you tell me where you saw “MCP” (coding, TikTok, school, etc.), I can narrow it down exactly.
